reviewFocus: Apply the generic high-stakes analysis review dimensions to household stock, consume-by, recipe, chore, and reorder claims.
Flag unsupported safety, spoilage, allergy, emergency-readiness, or household-action claims when synthetic evidence is weak or missing.
Check that evidence labels are traceable without exposing real household members, addresses, device names, shopping accounts, private source paths, or caller-owned implementation policy.
Keep real sensor integrations, notification routing, health decisions, purchasing, disposal, and operational thresholds caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not decide that an item is safe to consume, must be discarded, should be purchased, or requires emergency action.
Do not infer real household habits, diets, medical needs, budgets, vendor accounts, device integrations, storage conditions, or address-level context.
Do not treat this fixture as a product recommendation, food-safety instruction, medical instruction, purchase approval, or production alert policy.
